reasoning: The informal statement asserts that for any element x in a group G, x and x⁻¹ have the same order. The Lean theorem states `orderOf x = orderOf x⁻¹` for an arbitrary group `G` (with `[Group G]`) and an arbitrary element `x : G`. Both the hypothesis (x is an element of a group) and the conclusion (equality of orders) match exactly. Mathlib even has `orderOf_inv : orderOf x⁻¹ = orderOf x` which confirms this is the standard formalization. The implicit universal quantification over G and x via auto-bound type/variable universes captures the "for x an element in G" phrasing correctly. This is a faithful formalization.
